gates: strict selector leaf compile with warnings as errors

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (D : Nat) -> (H : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel H.Adj] -> (J : Finset (Sym2 V)) -> InAuxiliaryClass D H J -> HasValidRainbowColoring D H J
verified_results.scope: arbitrary finite vertex type and every formal member of InAuxiliaryClass
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists a proper edge assignment on H with colors Fin (D + 2) whose colors are pairwise distinct on distinguishedEdgeSet H J.

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {W : Type v} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> {H : SimpleGraph W} -> [Fintype W] -> [DecidableEq W] -> (D : Nat) -> (X : Auxiliary.Extension G H) -> [DecidableRel H.Adj] -> (J : Finset (Sym2 W)) -> (forall vertex : V, X.classEdge vertex in distinguishedEdgeSet H J) -> InAuxiliaryClass D H J -> exists assignment : Assignment G (ExtensionPalette D), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: arbitrary original graph G with a supplied conflict-preserving extension into a finite formal auxiliary-class member H and explicit selector membership in J
verified_results.output_kind: propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (D + 2)) with assignment.Valid; the theorem does not construct the extension or identify D with the maximum degree of G.

verified_results.availability: proof branch only; not yet on the default branch
verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (G : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> {q : Nat} -> 0 < q -> (phi : EdgeAssignment G (Fin q)) -> phi.Valid -> (A : Finset V) -> G.IsIndepSet (A : Set V) -> IndependentSeedPeelCertificate G A q -> exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (q + 1)), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: a finite simple graph with decidable vertex equality and adjacency, a positive old-palette size q, a supplied proper Fin q edge assignment, a supplied independent seed A, and a supplied peel certificate covering exactly the vertices outside A
verified_results.output_kind: conditional propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (q + 1)) with assignment.Valid. The peel certificate supplies a duplicate-free order covering exactly V minus A and the strict later-neighbor bound at every step. The proof reverse-colors that order using old-palette colors missing from incident edges, while A receives the one fresh color.
verified_results.maximum_degree_specialization: Setting q = G.maxDegree + 1 gives palette Fin (G.maxDegree + 2), hence a conditional Delta + 2 conclusion.
verified_results.maximum_degree_wrapper_signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (G : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> (phi : EdgeAssignment G (Fin (G.maxDegree + 1))) -> phi.Valid -> (A : Finset V) -> G.IsIndepSet (A : Set V) -> IndependentSeedPeelCertificate G A (G.maxDegree + 1) -> exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (G.maxDegree + 2)), assignment.Valid
verified_results.maximum_degree_wrapper_boundary: The wrapper only specializes q in the generic endpoint. The proper edge assignment, its validity, the independent seed, its independence proof, and the peel certificate remain explicit inputs.
declaration_local_missing_inputs: existence of a proper EdgeAssignment G (Fin (G.maxDegree + 1)); the library does not prove Vizing's theorem
existence of an independent seed A with an IndependentSeedPeelCertificate G A (G.maxDegree + 1)
verified_results.claim_boundary: The declaration is not an unrestricted Total Coloring Conjecture theorem and does not strengthen the unconditional scope of TotalColoring.exists_valid_assignment_of_highDegree.

verified_results.availability: proof branch only; not yet on the default branch
verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (G : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> {q : Nat} -> (phi : EdgeAssignment G (Fin q)) -> phi.Valid -> (S : Finset V) -> G.IsIndepSet (S : Set V) -> (F : Finset G.edgeSet) -> EdgeFinsetIsMatching F -> EdgeFinsetAvoids F S -> (K : Finset V) -> (g0 : V -> Fin q) -> SelectorOldColoringGoodOn G phi S F K g0 -> SelectorCorePeelCertificate G S K q -> exists assignment : Assignment G (Fin (q + 1)), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: a finite simple graph with decidable vertex equality and adjacency, a supplied proper Fin q edge assignment, an independent fresh-color vertex selector S, a matching fresh-color edge selector F avoiding S, a supplied actual-list old coloring on the core K minus S, and a supplied core-relative peel certificate
hypotheses: SelectorOldColoringGoodOn G phi S F K g0
cert : SelectorCorePeelCertificate G S K q
verified_results.peel_inequality: (G.neighborFinset v intersect ((K minus S) union tail.toFinset)).card < q - G.degree v
verified_results.output_kind: conditional propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: Vertices in S and edges in F receive the fresh color. The supplied core coloring and reverse greedy peel give allowed proper old colors to every other vertex, yielding a valid Assignment G (Fin (q + 1)).
verified_results.maximum_degree_specialization: Setting q = G.maxDegree + 1 gives palette Fin (G.maxDegree + 2), hence a conditional Delta + 2 conclusion.
verified_results.maximum_degree_wrapper_boundary: The proper edge assignment, independent set, matching selector, avoidance proof, actual-list core coloring, and core-relative peel certificate remain explicit inputs.
path_certificate_fields: k + 1 indexed injective vertices and k indexed edges
the path vertices span the supplied core
a globally unused old spare color
matching proofs for both alternating edge families
fresh edges avoid the path start
successor core colors equal predecessor edge colors
a SelectorCorePeelCertificate rooted at the path start
verified_results.path_wrapper_boundary: The wrapper proves the donor exchange and decoding from the explicit certificate; it does not prove existence of the proper edge coloring, selector/core, peel certificate, or alternating rainbow path.
declaration_local_missing_inputs: existence of a proper EdgeAssignment G (Fin (G.maxDegree + 1)); the library does not prove Vizing's theorem
existence of compatible selector vertex and edge sets
existence of a proper actual-list core coloring and core-relative peel certificate
existence of the indexed alternating rainbow-path certificate
verified_results.claim_boundary: The declarations are conditional decoders, not an unrestricted Total Coloring Conjecture theorem, and they do not strengthen the unconditional scope of TotalColoring.exists_valid_assignment_of_highDegree.

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (P : Auxiliary.PairSingletonWitness G) -> (v : V) -> P.extension.classEdge v in distinguishedEdgeSet P.auxiliaryGraph P.distinguished
verified_results.scope: a supplied PairSingletonWitness on a finite original vertex type
verified_results.output_kind: concrete auxiliary graph and conflict-preserving Extension data, selector membership, qualitative auxiliary-class structure, exact degree and class-count identities, and conditional high-degree structural-class membership
verified_results.conditional_class_conclusion: IsAuxiliaryClassMember D P.auxiliaryGraph none P.distinguished P.matchingPart
conditional_class_hypotheses: 2 <= P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none and P.auxiliaryGraph.degree none <= D
verified_results.high_degree_signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (P : Auxiliary.PairSingletonWitness G) -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> [DecidableRel P.auxiliaryGraph.Adj] -> P.distinguished.card = G.maxDegree + 1 -> Fintype.card V <= 2 * G.maxDegree -> IsAuxiliaryClassMember (G.maxDegree + 1) P.auxiliaryGraph none P.distinguished P.matchingPart
verified_results.high_degree_scope: a supplied P : PairSingletonWitness G on a finite original vertex type, with decidable vertex equality and decidable adjacency for G and P.auxiliaryGraph; beyond these ambient typeclass requirements, the class-count equality and density inequality are the only additional proposition-valued hypotheses; no partition or nonempty hypothesis is part of this wrapper
declaration_local_reduction_seams: this supplied-witness theorem does not construct its PairSingletonWitness input
this supplied-witness theorem does not itself select the empty-vertex branch
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: P.auxiliaryGraph is defined on Option V; P.extension is an Auxiliary.Extension G P.auxiliaryGraph; every selector lies in the corresponding distinguishedEdgeSet; each copied original vertex has exact distinguished-edge coverage; matchingPart is an off-center matching whose endpoints avoid center neighbors; and distinguished is matchingPart union the full center incidence star. Exact degree and center/class-count identities derive the former maximum-degree and center-degree obligations. The high-degree wrapper supplies IsAuxiliaryClassMember with parameter G.maxDegree + 1 from the listed class-count and density hypotheses. The separate checked EquitablePairSingleton adapter constructs P and proves J.card = D from a supplied equitable independent partition in the range D <= Fintype.card V < 2 * D. The project-level terminal theorem discharges witness construction and the empty branch by a different route.

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> {G : SimpleGraph V} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> [Nonempty V] -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> (Q : EquitableIndependentPartition G (G.maxDegree + 1)) -> Fintype.card V <= 2 * G.maxDegree -> exists assignment : Assignment G (ExtensionPalette (G.maxDegree + 1)), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: a nonempty finite graph G with decidable vertex equality and adjacency, an explicitly supplied equitable independent partition Q into exactly G.maxDegree + 1 nonempty classes, and the half-order density inequality
verified_results.output_kind: conditional propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: There exists assignment : Assignment G (ExtensionPalette (G.maxDegree + 1)) with assignment.Valid. This reusable conditional theorem constructs the pair/singleton witness, proves the exact distinguished count, establishes InAuxiliaryClass, and invokes the checked auxiliary-to-total transfer. By itself it does not construct Q or handle the empty graph; those project-level seams are discharged by TotalColoring.exists_valid_assignment_of_highDegree.
declaration_local_missing_inputs: existence of Q for every nonempty graph satisfying the intended high-degree hypotheses, potentially via a complement-matching construction
verified_results.project_level_status: discharged by the terminal high-degree theorem

verified_results.signature_summary: {V : Type u} -> [Fintype V] -> [DecidableEq V] -> (G : SimpleGraph V) -> [DecidableRel G.Adj] -> Fintype.card V <= 2 * G.maxDegree -> exists assignment : Assignment G (ExtensionPalette (G.maxDegree + 1)), assignment.Valid
verified_results.scope: every finite simple graph with decidable vertex equality and adjacency satisfying the half-order density inequality, including the empty vertex type and with no parity hypothesis
verified_results.output_kind: propositional existence of a semantically valid total-coloring assignment
verified_results.conclusion_expansion: The nonempty branch obtains an exact complement matching, builds the pair/singleton witness, enters the checked auxiliary class, and decodes a valid assignment. The empty branch uses a separate vacuous assignment theorem. This is a Lean-package theorem; it is not the author-locked paper theorem, a Delta + 2 theorem, the unrestricted Total Coloring Conjecture, or a novelty claim.
verified_results.publishable_tree_verification_status: passed a separate Wave 11 exact-tree gate, PR-head CI, and post-merge main CI; the external PR receipt names the certified tree without changing it

not_proved: an unrestricted total-coloring theorem for all finite graph orders
an unconditional high-degree or all-graphs total-coloring theorem with Delta + 2 colors
Vizing's theorem or universal existence of the proper edge-coloring witness required by the conditional independent-seed endpoint
universal existence of an independent seed and peel certificate satisfying the endpoint hypotheses
universal existence of a compatible total-independent selector, actual-list core coloring, core-relative peel certificate, or alternating rainbow-path certificate for the selector wrappers
an end-to-end Delta + 3 theorem without the half-order density hypothesis
an author-approved or manuscript-integrated paper theorem; the checked package declaration is not identified with the manuscript theorem
an executable total-coloring construction or a complexity bound
the equality chi''(G) = Delta(G) + 3 or use of every palette color; the theorem gives existence into a palette of that cardinality
trust_boundary: Lean 4.32.0 kernel checking is authoritative for the formal declarations.
Production Lean modules prohibit sorry, admit, custom axiom declarations, and native_decide.
Executable certificate checkers have semantic soundness theorems.
The independent-seed endpoint is a semantic existence theorem conditional on all of its supplied witnesses; it is not a witness generator for Vizing or the peel certificate.

The total-independent selector and alternating-path wrappers are semantic existence theorems conditional on every displayed edge-coloring, selector, core, peel, and path witness; they do not generate those witnesses.
External JSON parsing and claims of census completeness remain outside the trusted Lean interface.
A checked positive assignment validates that assignment; it does not prove an external enumeration complete.

the diagnostic trust script used an older cache missing a private Mathlib artifact
